title: Goertek-Backed AR Optical Wafer Plant Begins Operations in Shanghai's Lingang Area
gist: China's first large-scale 12-inch transparent-substrate wafer facility for AR micro-nano optics, run by Goertek OmniLight Optical Technology in Shanghai's Lingang area, reached commercial production in June 2026 after a CNY 3.28 billion (USD 457 million) investment, positioning Goertek to vertically integrate AR waveguide lens manufacturing as it bets on AI smart glasses as a priority growth area.

themes.theme: China's first large-scale AR optical wafer fab comes online

insights.claim: China's first large-scale 12-inch transparent-substrate wafer facility dedicated to AR micro-nano optical products officially started operations in Shanghai's Lingang area, with total investment of approximately CNY 3.28 billion (USD 457 million) across 261,000 square meters.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki (Trendforce)
insights.so_what: Marks a concrete, quantified capacity addition (CNY 3.28B/$457M, 261,000 sqm) to China's AR optics supply chain — a milestone for tracking domestic AR/XR component self-sufficiency and Goertek's vertical integration strategy.
insights.quote: Total investment in the project is approximately CNY 3.28 billion (USD 457 million), with the facility occupying a gross floor area of 261,000 square meters in Lingang's Blue Bay Industrial Zone.

insights.claim: The project moved from signing to commercial production in about three years and four months: signed February 2023, first building topped out September 2024, first core lithography tool installed July 2025, and commercial production reached June 2026.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki (Trendforce)
insights.so_what: Provides a concrete fab-buildout timeline benchmark (~40 months signing-to-production) for a specialized optical wafer facility, useful for modeling future capacity ramp timing in the AR/XR optics supply chain.
insights.quote: The project moved from signing to commercial production in about three years and four months.

insights.claim: The facility is run by Goertek OmniLight Optical Technology, formerly Sunny OmniLight NanoOptics and affiliated with Sunny Optical, after Goertek Optics took full ownership via a roughly CNY 530 million capital increase in 2025.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki (Trendforce)
insights.so_what: Documents Goertek's acquisition path (buying out Sunny Optical's stake in the AR optics unit) as evidence of consolidation and vertical integration in China's AR component supply chain.
insights.quote: In 2025, Goertek Optics, a subsidiary of Goertek, took full ownership of Sunny OmniLight via a capital increase of roughly CNY 530 million, folding the business into its precision optics operations.

themes.theme: Goertek's bet on AI smart glasses and AR/VR vertical integration

insights.claim: The facility supports diffractive waveguide design, precision silicon carbide processing, and nanoimprint lithography to produce AR waveguide lenses, electrochromic lenses, and microlens arrays, applying semiconductor-level manufacturing precision to AR optical devices.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki (Trendforce)
insights.so_what: Signals that AR waveguide optics manufacturing is converging with semiconductor-grade process technology (SiC processing, nanoimprint lithography) — relevant for assessing entry barriers and quality benchmarks in the AR component supply chain.
insights.quote: the facility supports diffractive waveguide design, precision silicon carbide processing, and nanoimprint lithography, producing high-end optical components such as AR waveguide lenses, electrochromic lenses, and microlens arrays

insights.claim: Goertek's 2025 annual report identifies AI smart glasses, mixed reality, augmented reality, and automotive electronics as priority growth areas, and the company is expanding into edge AI hardware while tracking large AI models and intelligent agents.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki (Trendforce)
insights.so_what: Confirms Goertek's strategic pivot toward AI-enabled wearables (smart glasses, MR/AR/VR) as a named growth priority, positioning the new wafer fab as upstream capacity to support that bet.
insights.quote: In its 2025 annual report, Goertek said it would track developments in large AI models and intelligent agents while expanding into edge AI hardware, citing AI smart glasses, mixed reality (MR), augmented reality (AR), and automotive electronics as priority growth areas.

insights.claim: In January 2026, Goertek began Phase II construction of its VR assembly production base (planned CNY 1.6 billion investment) and Goertek Optics signed a joint-venture agreement with Conant Optical to co-develop AI/AR/VR/MR eyewear products including customized prescription lenses and optical waveguide eyepieces.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki (Trendforce)
insights.so_what: Shows Goertek building both upstream (wafer fab) and downstream (assembly, JV with Conant Optical) capacity simultaneously — a full vertical-integration play in AI/AR/VR eyewear worth tracking against Meta, Apple, and other XR device makers' supply chains.
insights.quote: construction began on Phase II of Goertek's VR assembly production base, a project with planned investment of CNY 1.6 billion intended to expand capacity for VR headsets and AI-enabled smart glasses.
insights.locator: paragraph on Phase II VR base
